text.text:
*AI WEEKLY SIGNALS — 4–10 MAY 2026*

*Adoption:*
— :tada: AI-assisted hosted events: *25.0%* (WoW +1.7pp). 12-week climb: 18.1% → 25.0%. All-time high.
— Prompts per user (Slides Agent): *3.4* (WoW +0.3). 4w trend: trending up.

*Satisfaction:*
— :warning: Slides Agent CSAT: 74.5% (WoW -4.0pp), n=55. -6.0pp vs 4w avg of 80.6%.
— Slides Agent top comment: _"not useful"_ — said 4 times this week (of 7 comments).
— Presentation Review CSAT: 50.0% (n=4 — too sparse to read).
— Presentation Review pain points (last 90d, 20 comments): false-positives dominate — many users report flagged "issues" that aren't issues. A vocal minority want the old Review back: _"Bring back the old Review. This new version is terrible. I have 10 things highlighted and none are issues."_ Remainder split between feature requests and formatting bugs.

*Reliability:*
— Slides Agent failure-refreshes: flat WoW, trending down over 4w — good direction.
— Upload failure rate: down 0.8pp WoW, holding steady over 4w — good direction.

*Notable:*
— AI-assist crossed 25% for the first time — a 12-week climb from 18.1%. Adoption ticking up even as total hosted volume softened (~19.7k sessions vs ~25k earlier in April).
— Slides Agent CSAT slipped to 74.5%, -6.0pp vs the 4w average. Sample size (n=55) is modest but the drop is real, and the top free-text comment was, fittingly, _"not useful"_.
— PR CSAT is unreadable at n=4. The 90-day complaint pool tells the real story: 20 substantive comments, dominated by false-positives and nostalgia for the old Review.
